recipes.name: Baby Apple Scones
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 109 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: all-purpose flour (½ cup / 2½ oz), whole-wheat flour (½ cup / 2¾ oz), baking powder (1½ tsp), sugar (2 Tbsp), salt (¼ tsp), unsalted butter (3 Tbsp, chilled and cut into ¼-inch pieces), heavy cream (½ cup), dried apples (½ cup, chopped fine)
recipes.method: oven 425°F middle rack, parchment-lined baking sheet → process flours + baking powder + sugar + salt ~10 sec → add butter, pulse ~6 times until coarse meal → add cream, pulse 6–8 times until dough forms large clumps → add apples, pulse ~4 times → transfer to lightly floured counter, knead by hand 5–10 sec → pat into 12½×2½-inch rectangle → cut into 5 squares, then each diagonally → 10 triangles → transfer to sheet → refrigerate 10–15 min until chilled → bake 10–12 min until lightly browned, rotating halfway → cool on wire rack ≥10 min
recipes.storage: room temp up to 3 days OR freeze up to 1 month. To serve warm: 300°F 8–10 min.
recipes.notes: reduced-sugar healthier scone. Dried apples avoid excess moisture from fresh.

recipes.name: Brown Rice Balls with Spinach and Edamame
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 98–99 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: water (1 cup), short-grain brown rice (½ cup), baby spinach (½ cup), frozen shelled edamame (⅓ cup, thawed and patted dry), scallion (1, sliced thin), nori sheet (1 × 8×7.5-inch, crumbled, optional), toasted sesame oil (2 tsp), grated fresh ginger (½ tsp, or sub ¼ tsp ground), salt (⅛ tsp)
recipes.method: boil water + rice → cover, simmer low 30–35 min until tender → off heat, towel under lid, rest 10 min → pulse spinach + edamame + scallion + nori + sesame oil + ginger + salt in food processor ~12 pulses until finely ground (not smooth) → add rice, pulse ~8 more times until rice coarsely chopped → divide into 12 portions (~1½ Tbsp each), roll with lightly moistened hands
recipes.storage: refrigerate up to 3 days OR freeze up to 1 month
recipes.notes: inspired by Japanese onigiri but food-processor shortcut. Short-grain brown rice for cohesion.
recipes.serveWith: low-sodium soy sauce for dipping OR squeeze of fresh lemon/lime

recipes.name: Cheesy Rigatoni
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 100–101 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: water (2¼ cups), rigatoni (1 cup), unsalted butter (1 Tbsp), salt (⅛ tsp), grated Parmesan cheese (¼ cup)
recipes.method: bring water + rigatoni + butter + salt to vigorous simmer in 10-inch skillet medium-high → cook stirring frequently 14–16 min until pasta tender and almost all liquid evaporated → off heat, sprinkle Parmesan over → cover, rest 5 min → uncover, stir to emulsify
recipes.notes: must add Parmesan OFF heat. Use exactly a 10-inch skillet (ratio matters). Avoid pre-shredded Parmesan.
extras: makes about 1½ cups, 30 min total time

recipes.name: Chicken, Carrot, and Brown Rice
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 67 ("Adventurous Combinations")
recipes.ingredients: water (2½ cups), boneless skinless chicken thighs (2 × 3-oz, trimmed, cut into 1-inch pieces), carrots (3, 8 oz, peeled, cut into 1-inch pieces), long-grain brown rice (⅓ cup)
recipes.method: boil everything → cover, simmer medium-low ~40 min until very tender → potato-masher to porridge consistency, ~1 min → thin with water (or breast milk/formula) if needed
recipes.notes: brown rice picked for nutrient density (vs. white); thighs picked to handle the 40-min simmer without drying
recipes.spiceItUp: 1 tsp dried thyme with chicken in step 1, OR 1 Tbsp chopped fresh parsley before mashing

recipes.name: French Toast Fingers with Quick Berry Syrup
recipes.ingredients: thick-sliced day-old bread (5 slices, cut into wide strips), canned coconut milk (1 cup), ground flaxseed (1 Tbsp), vanilla (1 tsp), cinnamon (1 tsp), coconut oil or nondairy butter for pan; serve with Quick Berry Syrup
recipes.method: whisk milk + flax + vanilla + cinnamon, rest 5 min → dip bread strips → pan-fry 1–2 min per side until golden
recipes.notes: use thick sturdy bread; flax acts as egg replacer/binder

recipes.name: Frittata Bites
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. TBD
recipes.ingredients: large eggs (4), half-and-half (2 Tbsp), salt (pinch), extra-virgin olive oil (2 tsp), small Yukon Gold potato (1, 6 oz, peeled, quartered lengthwise, sliced thin), shallot (1, chopped), grated Parmesan (2 Tbsp)
recipes.method: oven 425°F → spray 6-cup muffin tin → whisk eggs + half-and-half + salt, set aside → in 10-inch nonstick skillet, heat oil medium, add potato + shallot + salt, cover, medium-low 10–12 min until potato tender → cool 2 min, stir in Parmesan → divide potato mix among 6 muffin cups → distribute egg mixture over → bake 8–10 min until lightly puffed and set → cool on wire rack 10 min, run butter knife around edges, lift out
recipes.storage: airtight container, refrigerate up to 24 hrs
extras: makes 6 mini frittatas, 45 min total time

recipes.name: Glazed Tofu Sticks
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. TBD
recipes.ingredients: firm tofu (2 × 1-inch-thick slabs from 14-oz block, sliced into 6 sticks), low-sodium soy sauce (1½ tsp), maple syrup (1½ tsp), lemon juice (1 tsp), ground ginger (⅛ tsp), canola oil (1 tsp)
recipes.method: slice tofu into 6 sticks → drain on paper towels 20 min, press dry → mix soy + maple + lemon + ginger in small bowl → heat oil in 8-inch nonstick skillet medium-high → cook tofu golden + crisp on all 4 sides, ~2 min per side → off heat, add sauce, gently toss to coat
recipes.storage: refrigerate up to 2 days, serve room temp or microwave 15 sec
recipes.notes: crispy outside, moist inside, light sweet-savory-tangy glaze.

recipes.name: Mini Meatballs
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 103 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: hearty white sandwich bread (2 slices, crusts removed, torn into 1-inch pieces), whole milk (¼ cup), extra-virgin olive oil (1 Tbsp), small onion (1, chopped fine), salt (½ tsp), garlic cloves (2, minced), dried oregano (1 tsp), grated Parmesan (1 oz / ½ cup), large egg (1, lightly beaten), minced fresh parsley (2 Tbsp), lean ground beef (1 lb, 85% lean)
recipes.method: oven 450°F upper-middle rack, foil-lined sheet pan, spray with oil → mash bread + milk with fork to smooth paste → heat oil in 10-inch skillet medium, cook onion + salt 5–7 min until softened and lightly browned → add garlic + oregano, cook 30 sec until fragrant → transfer onion mix to bread bowl → stir in Parmesan + egg + parsley → add beef, gently mix → divide into 24 portions (~1½ Tbsp each), roll → arrange on sheet → bake 18–20 min, rotating sheet halfway
recipes.storage: refrigerate up to 3 days OR freeze up to 1 month. Reheat at 400°F until sizzling ~15 min.
recipes.notes: panade (bread+milk paste) keeps meatballs moist. Designed as finger food. *Big iron win — 85% lean beef is one of the densest heme iron sources for Baby.*
recipes.serveWith: Hand-Squished Roasted Tomato Sauce (p. 213) OR plain

recipes.name: PBJ's Blender Bean Muffins
recipes.ingredients: rolled oats (⅔ cup), canned chickpeas (15 oz, drained + rinsed), overripe banana (1 medium), sunflower seed butter (¼ cup), kale (1 cup loosely chopped), unsweetened soy milk (½ cup), cinnamon (¼ tsp), vanilla (1 tsp), baking powder (1 tsp)
recipes.method: process oats to flour → add everything → blend → fill cups → bake 350°F, 20 min
recipes.notes: palmar-grasp-friendly, naturally sweetened, iron + protein dense.

recipes.name: Quick Berry Syrup
recipes.ingredients: frozen berries (2 cups, slightly thawed), water (⅓ cup), cinnamon (½ tsp), vanilla (½ tsp)
recipes.method: combine in saucepan, bring to boil, smash large berries gently, simmer ~10 min until thick
recipes.notes: "syrup without the sugar" — designed as dip for French Toast Fingers; works on pancakes, oatmeal, yogurt

recipes.name: Raw Fruit and Nut Bars
recipes.primaryIngredient: Dried Fruit (cherries / apricots / cranberries / dates / raisins)
searchTerms: dried fruit (cherries / apricots / cranberries / dates / raisins)
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 111 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: hot water (1 cup), dried cherries OR apricots OR cranberries (¾ cup, or mix of 2–3), pitted dates (3 oz / ½ cup, chopped), raw whole almonds (1 cup), ground cinnamon (¼ tsp), salt (¼ tsp)
recipes.method: line 8½×4½-inch loaf pan with plastic wrap (excess hanging over) → combine water + cherries + dates, let soften 5–10 min → drain well, pat dry → process almonds + cinnamon + salt ~20 sec until finely ground → add drained fruit, pulse 15–20 times until very finely chopped and mixture starts to clump → transfer to pan, spread even, fold plastic over, press flat → refrigerate ~1 hour until firm → slice in half lengthwise, then each half crosswise into 6 pieces → 12 bars
recipes.notes: homemade alternative to Larabar / commercial fruit-and-nut bars. **Dairy-free, no-bake, naturally sweetened.** Save the dried fruit choice for what's on sale / on hand.
extras: makes 12 small bars, 15 min + 1 hr chilling

recipes.name: Red Lentil, Carrot, and Coconut Milk
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 59 ("Adventurous Combinations")
recipes.ingredients: dried red lentils (⅔ cup), carrots (3, 8 oz, peeled, sliced ½-inch thick), water (1¾ cups), unsweetened canned coconut milk (¼ cup)
recipes.method: boil everything → cover, simmer low 20–25 min until very tender → potato-masher to porridge consistency → thin with water (or breast milk/formula) if needed
recipes.notes: dal-inspired. Red lentils break down soft and pulpy. Second-highest protein-to-calorie ratio of any legume. *Uses red lentils specifically.*
recipes.spiceItUp: ¼ tsp ground ginger OR ¼ tsp ground turmeric with lentils in step 1

recipes.name: Salmon, Pea, and Rice
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 75 ("Adventurous Combinations")
recipes.ingredients: long-grain white rice (⅓ cup, rinsed), water (1¾ cups), grated orange zest (¼ tsp) + juice (2 Tbsp), skinless salmon fillet (1 × 6-oz, cut into 1-inch pieces), frozen peas (⅓ cup)
recipes.method: boil rice + water + orange juice → cover, simmer low 10 min → add salmon + peas, simmer 6–8 min until very tender → off heat, add orange zest, potato-masher to porridge, ~1 min → thin if needed
recipes.notes: **DO NOT blender — rice goes gummy.** Stick with potato masher. Orange is the surprise flavor anchor.
recipes.spiceItUp: 2 tsp fresh mint OR 1 tsp fresh tarragon, added with orange zest

recipes.name: Savory Oatcakes
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 105 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: water (⅓ cup), unsalted butter (3 Tbsp, melted and cooled), quick oats (1 cup / 3 oz), all-purpose flour (⅓ cup / 1⅔ oz), sugar (2 tsp), baking powder (¾ tsp), salt (½ tsp)
recipes.method: oven 350°F middle rack, parchment-lined baking sheet → whisk water + melted butter → whisk oats + flour + sugar + baking powder + salt → combine wet + dry, stir until well combined (sticky) → rest dough 10 min → scrape onto floured counter, pat into 8-inch circle, ¼ inch thick → cut 12 rounds with 2-inch biscuit cutter (gather and re-press scraps) → transfer to sheet → bake ~25 min until golden brown, rotating halfway → cool on sheet on wire rack ~30 min
recipes.notes: traditional Scottish biscuits. Quick oats are already broken down, no food processor needed.

recipes.name: Sunshine Bowl
recipes.ingredients: silken tofu (½ cup, drained), carrots (¼ cup steamed ~10 min), strawberries (¼ cup fresh or thawed), mango chunks (¼ cup), chia seeds (1 tsp)
recipes.method: steam carrots ~10 min; blend everything to puree
recipes.familyTip: top with nuts, seeds, extra fruit

recipes.name: Tiny Carrot Muffins
secondaryIngredients: Dried Fruit (cherries / apricots / cranberries / dates / raisins)
searchTerms: dried fruit (cherries / apricots / cranberries / dates / raisins)
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 107 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: flour (1 cup / 5½ oz — recipe calls for whole-wheat; uses all-purpose), baking powder (¾ tsp), baking soda (½ tsp), ground cinnamon (½ tsp), ground ginger (½ tsp), salt (¼ tsp), plain yogurt (½ cup — uses Forager), packed brown sugar (⅓ cup / 2⅓ oz), canola oil (¼ cup), large eggs (2), vanilla extract (½ tsp), shredded carrots (1 cup / 2 small carrots)
recipes.method: oven 375°F middle rack → grease 24-cup mini muffin tin → whisk dry (flour + baking powder + baking soda + cinnamon + ginger + salt) → whisk wet (yogurt + sugar + oil + eggs + vanilla) → stir carrots into wet until incorporated → add dry, stir just until combined → divide batter (~1 heaping Tbsp per cup) → bake 10–12 min until toothpick clean → cool in tin on rack 10 min, then on rack ~30 min
recipes.storage: room temp up to 3 days OR freeze up to 1 month. To serve warm: 300°F 8–10 min.
recipes.notes: carrots are 88% water → contribute fluffy texture. Standing subs (yogurt → Forager, flour → all-purpose) make this dairy-free without thought.

recipes.name: Zucchini-Carrot Fritters
recipes.source: ATK Complete Baby & Toddler Cookbook, p. 88–89 ("Finger Foods")
recipes.ingredients: zucchini (1 small, shredded, ~1 cup), carrots (2 small, peeled and shredded, ~1 cup), salt (¼ tsp), large egg (1, lightly beaten), all-purpose flour (2 Tbsp), extra-virgin olive oil (1 Tbsp)
recipes.method: toss zucchini + carrots with salt, drain 10 min in fine-mesh strainer over bowl → wrap in clean dish towel, squeeze out as much liquid as possible → mix with egg → sprinkle flour, stir → heat oil in 12-inch nonstick skillet medium → drop 2-Tbsp portions, press into 2-inch fritters → cook 3 min per side until golden brown → drain on paper towels
recipes.notes: shredding + squeezing is essential for non-soggy fritters.

ingredients.name: Dried Fruit (cherries / apricots / cranberries / dates / raisins)
